<!-- Topic 5: Parallel Arrays -->
<!-- Total slides: 6 -->

# Parallel Arrays

---

## Multiple arrays, one index? {.smaller}

- You've seen parallel vectors — parallel arrays are the same pattern
- One shared index ties corresponding elements across multiple arrays

::: notes
Total slides: 6
:::

<!-- Slide 1 -->

---

## What are parallel arrays?

Three separate arrays, one student per index:

```
index:    0        1        2        3
names:  "Alice"  "Bob"   "Carol"  "Dave"
scores:   91       78       84       95
grades:  'A'      'C'      'B'      'A'
```

- `names[2]`, `scores[2]`, and `grades[2]` all describe Carol
- The index is the only link between the arrays

::: notes
[Graphic suggestion: three horizontal arrays stacked vertically, each cell aligned by column, with vertical lines connecting index 2 across all three arrays to highlight the shared index relationship]
:::

<!-- Slide 2 -->

---

## Declaration

```{.cpp}
const int SIZE = 4;

string names[SIZE]  = {"Alice", "Bob", "Carol", "Dave"};
int    scores[SIZE] = {91, 78, 84, 95};
char   grades[SIZE] = {'A', 'C', 'B', 'A'};
```

- All arrays share the same `SIZE` constant — use one named constant for all of them
- Adding or removing a student requires updating every array

<!-- Slide 3 -->

---

## Access and display

```{.cpp}
for (int i = 0; i < SIZE; i++) {
    cout << names[i] << "  "
         << scores[i] << "  "
         << grades[i] << "\n";
}
```

Output:
```
Alice  91  A
Bob    78  C
Carol  84  B
Dave   95  A
```

<!-- Slide 4 -->

---

## Parallel arrays vs. structs

- Parallel arrays keep related data apart — updating one record means touching every array
- A struct keeps all fields for one record together in one place
- Use parallel arrays for simple, short-lived data; prefer structs when records need to be inserted, deleted, or passed around

<!-- Slide 5 -->

---

## Summary

- Parallel arrays share a common index — `arr1[i]`, `arr2[i]`, `arr3[i]` all describe the same entity
- All arrays must use the same size constant; every structural change must be applied consistently across all of them

<!-- Slide 6 -->
